[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2024-08-26
[TITLE]: Valley turns out with Pride - Alamosa Citizen
[METADATA/INTRO]: They can take the flags but they can’t take pride and happiness. Early on Aug. 24, just hours before the pride parade was set to march down Main Street, someone stole every pride flag lining Main Street. Three of them...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Color and smiles marched along on a sunny Main Street. Some small crowds gathered to cheer and wave, sporting pride in their own ways. Brushing their teeth, one onlooker stood on the sidewalk in front of their apartment, smiled a frothy smile and waved. 
 Dogs marched alongside on leashes. Astride a human chariot, a colorful parrot joined in – also on a leash. 
 The parade found its way to Cole Park where the festivities continued all day. With vendors, drag shows, live music, free support services from health care providers, and visits from local politicians, SLV Pride Fest adds itself to Alamosa’s growing list of vibrant and popular community events. This one is a little more low-key, perhaps, but it might be the loudest.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2022-06-12
[TITLE]: More than 100 rally Saturday for Longmont Pride Visibility March – Boulder Daily Camera
[METADATA/INTRO]: In a pair of black six-inch heels and knee-high rainbow stockings Aunuli Mansfield marched and sometimes jogged alongside more than 100 people who paraded several blocks during a visibility march through downtown Longmont. She joined chants of “Celebrate your Pride....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: In a pair of black six-inch heels and knee-high rainbow stockings Aunuli Mansfield marched and sometimes jogged alongside more than 100 people who paraded several blocks during a visibility march through downtown Longmont. 
 She joined chants of “Celebrate your Pride. Love wins every time!” and “2,4,6,8, we’re queer! Stop the hate!” 
 The visibility march was one element of Out Boulder County’s Longmont Pride Festival on Saturday. Mansfield, who is trans and lesbian, said taking part in the march was important. 
 “I’ve done (the march) before,” Mansfield said. “Every time, I have a great time. It’s great to be here to support trans rights and LGBTQ rights.”

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2024-06-11
[TITLE]: Celebrate Pride and Community in Denver at These Events - 303 Magazine
[METADATA/INTRO]: Denver’s first pride celebration was held in Cheesman Park, 1974. It was quickly followed by the opening of The Center on Colfax in 1975 — a still-running nonprofit organization that supports and services the LGBTQ+ community. There are so many...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The parade will head west on Colfax, spanning 14 blocks and ending near Civic Center Park. It is free to attend the parade and nearly 100,000+ people are expected to be present, lining the streets. Witness the colorful floats, marchers, music and more along those 14 blocks of Colfax Ave. 
 Pride Month at HashTAG 
 HashTAG is offering “Drag Me To Pride” mimosa flights for the month of June, where guests can choose four rainbow-colored mimosas for $19, with a portion of the proceeds benefiting The Trevor Project. The mimosa options include red: pomegranate juice, orange: carrot juice, yellow: “fuzzy navel,” orange juice and peach schnapps, green: matcha lemonade, blue: blue citrus, and purple: butterfly pea flower.
